subject: fix(pwa): align SW update flow with synctone/few-lap/canva-grid patterns
body:
Cross-project review found 4 issues:

1. skipWaiting/prompt conflict: skipWaiting:true in workbox config
   auto-activated new SWs, causing controllerchange reload before the
   user ever saw the update prompt. Removed — now user-controlled via
   applyUpdate() which sends SKIP_WAITING message.

2. Surprise reloads: controllerchange listener reloaded on ANY SW
   takeover, including background lifecycle events. Added
   _userClickedUpdate guard so only user-initiated updates reload.

3. False re-detection: After reload from update, onNeedRefresh could
   fire again for the same SW version. Added 30s sessionStorage
   suppression window (pattern from synctone/few-lap).

4. No recovery: If SW serves corrupt assets, app hangs on spinner
   forever. Added 30s recovery script that clears caches and
   unregisters SW (max 2 attempts per session, pattern from
   synctone/few-lap).

Tags: pwa, resilience, cross-project
Complexity: 3
Urgency: 3
Impact: user-facing
Risk: medium
Debt: paid
Epic: pwa-hardening
Semver: minor

title: align SW update flow with synctone/few-lap/canva-grid patterns
